references.title: A Unified Framework for Human-Agent Collaboration
references.author: Microsoft Research
references.abstract: This paper proposes a unified framework for Human-Agent Collaboration, defining five levels of collaboration from tool use to full autonomy.

references.title: Towards fluid human-agent collaboration: From dynamic goal recognition to adaptive action selection
references.author: Chiari, M., et al.
references.abstract: This study explores dynamic human-agent collaboration, proposing the concept of 'fluid collaboration' that adapts to context changes.

references.title: LLM-Based Human-Agent Collaboration and Interaction Systems: A Survey
references.author: Peng, H., et al.
references.abstract: This paper provides the first comprehensive survey of LLM-based Human-Agent Collaboration systems, systematically presenting core components and interaction patterns.

references.title: Shaping the Future of Project Management With AI
references.author: PMI (Project Management Institute)
references.abstract: This report analyzes how AI is transforming project management and predicts the future role of project managers in AI-driven environments.

references.title: Agent2Agent Protocol Specification
references.author: Google
references.abstract: The A2A protocol enables AI agents to communicate with each other, securely exchange information, and coordinate actions across vendor boundaries.

references.title: Multi-Agent Collaboration via Cross-Team Orchestration
references.author: Khan, A., et al.
references.abstract: This paper proposes a cross-team multi-agent collaboration framework that enables multiple specialized agent teams to jointly solve complex problems.

references.title: Reflective Multi-Agent Collaboration based on Large Language Models
references.author: NeurIPS 2024
references.booktitle: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 37
references.abstract: This paper proposes a reflective multi-agent collaboration mechanism that allows agents to self-reflect during collaboration, improving task completion accuracy by 25%.

references.title: LLM-Based Multi-Agent Systems for Software Engineering: A Survey
references.author: Wang, L., et al.
references.abstract: This survey explores the transformative potential of integrating Large Language Models into Multi-Agent systems for addressing complex challenges in software engineering.

references.title: Multi-Agent Software Development through Cross-Team Collaboration
references.author: Chen, X., et al.
references.abstract: A scalable multi-team framework that enables orchestrated teams to jointly propose various decisions and communicate with their insights.

references.title: Facilitating Trustworthy Human-Agent Collaboration in LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems
references.author: Li, Y., et al.
references.abstract: This study addresses trust issues in human-agent collaboration, proposing calibrated trust mechanisms for LLM-based multi-agent systems.

references.title: Artificial Intelligence Tools for Project Management: A Conceptual Framework
references.author: Smith, J., et al.
references.abstract: This study proposes a conceptual framework for the integration of artificial intelligence tools in project management.

references.title: How Artificial Intelligence Will Transform Project Management in the Digital Age
references.author: Müller, R., et al.
references.abstract: The study highlights how AI-driven methodologies and tools can revolutionize the way project risks are managed throughout the project lifecycle.

references.title: Orchestrating Human-AI Teams: The Manager Agent as a Unifying Framework
references.author: ACM Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems (CHI 2025)
references.abstract: This paper presents a research vision for autonomous agentic systems that orchestrate collaboration within dynamic human-AI teams.

references.title: Measuring the Impact of Human-AI Collaboration on Knowledge Diffusion in Organizations
references.author: Zhang, M., et al.
references.abstract: This study proposes a comprehensive framework to explore the impact of human-AI collaboration on organizational knowledge diffusion.

references.title: A Comprehensive Review of AI-Driven Project Management Techniques in Software Development

references.title: Challenges of Integrating Artificial Intelligence in Software Project Management
references.author: Anderson, P., et al.
references.abstract: This study investigates key obstacles to applying artificial intelligence in project management, specifically in the project planning phase.

references.title: Multi-Agent AI Collaboration: Advancing Software Engineering with Autonomous LLMs
references.author: ResearchGate
references.abstract: This paper explores the role of autonomous LLMs in multi-agent AI collaboration, focusing on their impact on code generation, debugging, and project management.

references.title: BioSage: Human-Agent Collaboration Platform for Cross-Disciplinary Research

references.author: Lee, K., et al.

references.title: Designing Collaborative Multi-Agent Systems with the A2A Protocol
references.author: O'Reilly Media

references.title: Transformers in Medical Image Analysis: A Review

references.title: Attention Is All You Need

references.abstract: We propose a new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions entirely.

references.title: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners

references.abstract: We demonstrate that scaling up language models greatly improves task-agnostic, few-shot performance.
